data.title: Novartis Says Won't be Impacted by 100% Tariff on Branded Pharmaceutical Products
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Novartis (NVS) said Friday that the newly announced 100% tariff on branded or patented pharmaceutical products will not impact it as it continues to advance its plan to ensure key medicines for US patients are manufactured locally.

The statement comes after US President Donald Trump said that a 100% tariff would be imposed on branded or patented pharmaceutical products unless the owning company is building a manufacturing plant in the country.

"Our $23 billion investment in US-based infrastructure continues to progress as we work to ensure all key Novartis medicines for US patients will be made in the United States," the drugmaker said in an emailed statement to MT Newswires.

"We have ongoing construction and expect to announce five new sites to be under construction before end of year. We are well prepared with product supply in the US through mid-2026," the company also said.

data.title: Sanofi to Offer its Insulins for $35 a Month in US Starting Jan. 1, 2026
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Sanofi (SNY) said Friday that it is offering a 30-day supply of any Sanofi insulin for $35 to all patients in the US with a valid prescription, as part of its Insulins Valyou Savings Program.

The program will be available to all Americans regardless of insurance status, including those with commercial insurance or Medicare, Sanofi said.

The expanded Insulins Valyou Savings Program will be available to patients beginning January 1, 2026, the company added.

data.title: Alphabet's Google Likely to Receive Second EU Antitrust Fine in Coming Months
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- (Updates with statement from Google in the fifth and sixth paragraphs.)

Alphabet's (GOOG, GOOGL) Google is likely to be hit with a second fine in the coming months under the European Union's Digital Markets Act, Reuters reported Thursday, citing three people with direct knowledge of the matter.

The fine is related to charges that Google favored its search engines such as Google Shopping, Google Flights and Google Hotels over rivals, the report said.

The EU Commission is in no hurry to wrap up the case in light of recent criticism from the Trump administration of the EU's tech rules and subsequent penalties brought on American tech companies, the people said.

Google was recently hit with a 2.95 billion euro ($3.45 billion) fine from the EU for favoring its online display technology services and its ad exchange, Reuters said.

In a blog post Thursday, Google said that the Digital Markets Act is causing "significant and unintended harm" to European users by requiring Google Search to show links to intermediary websites instead of direct ones to airline and hotel sites, raising prices for consumers.

The EU regulation has also impacted European businesses and could cause them to lose up to 114 billion in revenue, Google said, citing a report by the Digital Markets Competition Forum of the Copenhagen Business School.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: BTQ Technologies Starts Trading on Nasdaq
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- BTQ Technologies (BTQ) said its common shares will start trading on the Nasdaq Global Market on Friday under the ticker symbol BTQ.

The quantum technology company's shares on the same day will stop trading on the OTCQX, where it was quoted under the symbol BTQQF, it said Friday.

BTQ Technologies also said its shares will continue to trade on the CBOE Canada and the Borse Frankfurt exchanges.

data.title: Royal Bank of Canada Eyeing US Acquisition Targets, CEO Dave McKay Says
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Royal Bank of Canada (RY) is keeping tabs on a number of possible US acquisition targets to expand its business south of the border, Bloomberg News reported Friday, citing Chief Executive Dave McKay.

"It wouldn't be asset management. It would be on the wealth-distribution side," McKay was quoted as saying by Bloomberg News. "We want more customers, we want that relationship."

McKay described the possible takeover targets as "high-quality wealth franchises" that could be sought by rivals.

"It would be, I think, a very aggressive bid for them by a number of players and, therefore, can you make the numbers work?" he was quoted as saying.

McKay said the company would also be open to wealth management deals in the UK and Asia, the report said.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Novartis Expects No Impact from New US Pharmaceutical Tariff
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Novartis (NOVN.SW) said Friday the US' plan to impose a 100% tariff on any branded or patented pharmaceutical product will not affect the company.

In a statement to MT Newswires, the Swiss drugmaker said work for its $23 billion investment in US infrastructure continues, with construction ongoing and five new sites expected before year-end.

US President Donald Trump announced Friday that the tariffs, effective Oct. 1, would apply unless companies are manufacturing their pharmaceutical products in America.

data.title: China Power International Electricity Sales Jump 4.8% in August
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Power International Development's (HKG:2380) sold over 11.7 million megawatt-hours of electricity in August, up 4.8% from a year prior, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Of the total, hydro, photovoltaic, and wind power sales jumped 35%, 22%, and 16%, respectively, to around 2.2 million megawatt-hours, 2.8 million megawatt-hours, and 1.7 million megawatt-hours.

Natural gas, coal-fired, and environmental power sales, on the other hand, slipped 22%, 13%, and 11% to 180,929 megawatt-hours, 4.7 million megawatt-hours, and 123,072 megawatt-hours.

From January to August, electricity sales fell 1.1% year over year to 86.8 million megawatt-hours.

data.title: Meta to Roll Out Ad-free Subscription for Facebook, Instagram in UK
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Meta Platforms (FB2A.F) will launch an ad-free subscription option for Facebook and Instagram in the UK "over the coming weeks," according to a Friday release.

The social media platform operator said the subscription will cost 2.99 pounds sterling per month for web users and 3.99 pounds for iOS and Android users, for the first Meta account. Those who stick with the free option will continue to see personalized ads.

Meta said the launch follows recent UK regulatory guidance and "extensive" engagement with the Information Commissioner's Office.

data.title: India to gross borrow 6.77 trillion rupees in October-March
data.description:
NEW DELHI, Sept 26 (Reuters) - The Indian government will raise 6.77 trillion rupees ($76.32 billion) through market borrowings during the October to March period, marginally lower than its previously announced schedule in its effort to tame rising bond yields and ensure market stability.

India had planned to raise 14.82 trillion rupees in the current financial year through March, and sold bonds worth 7.95 trillion rupees in April-September.

The government has reduce the share of ultra-long bonds to 29.5% in second half, down from 35% in first half, while it has also included 100 billion rupees of green bond sales.

($1 = 88.7110 Indian rupees) (Reporting by Nikunj Ohri and Dharamraj Dhutia)

data.title: Novartis Says U.S. Drug Tariffs Shouldn't Affect It
data.description:
By Billy Gray


Novartis said U.S. drug tariffs shouldn't affect the company since construction is under way on new manufacturing plants in the country.

The Swiss pharma group said Friday that it expects construction on five additional sites to start this year, after pledging to invest $23 billion in the U.S. in April.

The company's statement came after President Trump on Thursday said the U.S. will apply 100% tariffs, starting in October, to pharmaceuticals made by companies not building U.S. sites.


Write to Billy Gray at william.gray@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-26-25 0812ET

data.title: Keeper Security Integration with Google Security Operations Expands Visibility into Privileged Access
data.description:
Keeper Security announced a new integration with Google Security Operations. The integration streaming privileged access activity from Keeper into the Google Security Operations platform - which unifies threat detection, management and response with frontline intelligence and AI to help organizations stay ahead of new and emerging risks. As attackers increasingly leverage AI-driven techniques and sophisticated cyber campaigns, organizations must improve both visibility and speed of response.

By combining Keeper's Advanced Reporting and Alerts Module (ARAM) with Google Security Operations, security teams gain real-time, centralized insights into privileged access activity across their environments. Events are continuously streamed directly into Google Security Operations, helping to eliminate visibility gaps and reduce the burden of manual oversight. The result is faster, more precise detection and response to potential breaches.

Strengthening Enterprise Cyber Defenses: Real-time monitoring: Continuous event streaming from Keeper into Google Security Operations provides immediate detection of suspicious or unauthorized behavior; Operational efficiency: Automated reporting and alerting reduce manual log reviews, freeing IT and security teams to focus on strategic priorities; Regulatory compliance: Comprehensive event logging and access control documentation support audits for the GDPR, PCI DSS, SOC and ISO standards; Proactive protection: BreachWatch®? event data can be ingested to identify exposed credentials and prevent account takeover attempts. KeeperPAM®?'s unified, cloud-native platform secures passwords, passkeys, secrets and privileged sessions across hybrid and multi-cloud environments.

Leveraging agentic AI, KeeperPAM enables real-time threat detection and response through its KeeperAI feature, ensuring high-risk sessions are automatically terminated and all user activity is analyzed and categorized. By enforcing least-privilege access policies and delivering actionable intelligence from every privileged account, KeeperPAM reduces the risk of breaches and strengthens enterprise cybersecurity resilience.

Basic loss per share from continuing operations was HKD 6.82 compared to HKD 4.6 a year ago. Diluted loss per share from continuing operations was HKD 6.82 compared to HKD 4.6 a year ago.

data.title: VW to overhaul India business amid market pressures, company memo shows
data.description:
NEW DELHI (Reuters) -Volkswagen Group is restructuring its business in India, a key growth market for the carmaker where it wants to invest more but is grappling with policy changes and growing competition, according to an internal memo reviewed by Reuters.

The move comes as the company faces India's biggest-ever import tax demand of $1.4 billion for evading levies, and as its market share languishes despite more than two decades of operations in the world's third-largest car market.

Skoda Auto, a Volkswagen Group brand, has been leading the carmaker's India strategy since 2018.

The company has hired external experts to conduct a thorough review of its systems and processes and recommend improvements, Piyush Arora, chief of the local unit Skoda Auto Volkswagen India said in a memo sent to employees on September 8.

"Engaging a third party will provide a neutral perspective and some out-of-the-box ideas. I request you to support and cooperate with the team," he said in the memo, which was reviewed by Reuters.

The memo did not detail any changes on investment and jobs.

Skoda is deeply committed to the country and will invest in new technologies and manufacturing even as it faces shifting market trends and increasing competitive pressures, Arora added.

The exercise, which he said is the beginning of a "high performance organisation" journey and a course correction, coincides with the departure of close to 10 senior level executives at the carmaker over the past few weeks, two sources aware of the exits said.

This includes Nalin Jain, its finance chief and India board member; Sarma Chillara, head of human resources; Deepti Singh, head of external affairs; Hemant Malpani, head of cost control; and Shriniwas Chakravarthy, head of quality management, the two sources said, adding some resigned and some were asked to leave.

Skoda Auto Volkswagen India said that personnel changes correspond with standard company HR processes, without elaborating.

"India is a key market in Skoda Auto's internationalisation plans. We are always considering new business opportunities and are evaluating various options to ensure the best possible solution to implement our strategy in the highly dynamic Indian market," the company said in a statement.

Skoda is at a crucial point and needs to finalise its next leg of investment in India, a key market for the carmaker outside Europe given it no longer has a big presence in China and has exited Russia.

With stricter vehicle fuel efficiency norms set to kick in from 2027, all carmakers will have to introduce EVs and Skoda and VW currently do not sell any.

The company has plans to adapt Volkswagen's EV technology from China for India in which Skoda CEO Klaus Zellmer has previously said it will invest and is looking for a partner with "local roots". It has an agreement with India's Mahindra & Mahindra to supply some EV components.

The restructuring is to ensure the company is lean and agile to compete with nimbler rivals ahead of making new investments, said a third source with direct knowledge of the matter.

Despite being in the country for over two decades, the carmaker has struggled to become a significant player. Volkswagen and Skoda brands together account for just 2% of India's 4 million units a year car market lagging newer rival Kia and established players like Toyota.

Even as the carmaker's revenues in India have nearly tripled to $2.15 billion from about $766 million five years ago, its profit in India has dropped to $10.6 million from about $85 million over the same period, regulatory disclosures showed.

Skoda Auto Volkswagen is also embroiled in a legal tussle with India's tax department over allegations it misclassified imports of some Audi, VW and Skoda cars to evade higher duties.

If the company loses, it will need to fork out $2.8 billion including penalties and interest.

(Reporting by Aditi Shah; Editing by Kim Coghill)

By Aditi Shah

data.title: FutureCrest Acquisition Prices Initial Public Offering of 25 Million Units at $10 per Unit
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- FutureCrest Acquisition (FCRS), a special purpose acquisition company focused on artificial intelligence, digital assets, fintech, robotics and communications, said Friday it has priced its initial public offering of 25 million units at $10 each, for gross proceeds of $250 million.

The company expects its units to start trading on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol "FCRS.U" Friday, while the IPO is expected to close Sept. 29.

The IPO's underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to 3.75 million additional units at the IPO price to cover over-allotments, if any.

Once the securities begin separate trading, the shares will trade under the symbol "FCRS", while the warrants will trade under "FCRS WS," the company said.

Each unit consists of one class A ordinary share and one-quarter of one redeemable warrant, the company said. Each whole warrant entitles the holder to purchase one class A ordinary share at $11.50.

data.title: Fitch's Updated Bond Rating Criteria Has No Impact on Three Korean Programs
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Fitch Ratings' revised bond rating criteria has no rating effect on three Korean covered bond programs, according to a Friday release.

The rating agency applied the criteria to the AAA-rated covered bonds of KB Financial Group's (KRX:105560) Kookmin Bank, Hana Financial Group's (KRX:086790) KEB Hana Bank, and Shinhan Financial Group's (KRX:055550) Shinhan Bank.

In the revised criteria, Fitch considered a 25% haircut to its calculated breakeven overcollateralization for ratings of programs with a resolution reference point of at least BBB+, given the presence of eligible unencumbered assets.

The rating agency also no longer differentiates between issuers with support-driven and viability rating-linked issuer default ratings when extending a resolution uplift.

The resolution reference point for the Korean programs surpassed BBB+ by two notches, in line with the requirement for the 25% reduction, Fitch said.

The rating agency views the three issuers as having ample buffers, primarily due to regulations that limit covered bond issuances to 4% of total assets.

data.title: European Bourses Gain Midday Ahead of US Inflation Report
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- European bourses tracked modestly higher midday Friday as traders shrugged off new Trump Administration tariffs on pharmaceuticals and other goods, and awaited the latest inflation report from Washington.

Bank stocks led gainers, while tech shares lagged.

Investors also eyed muted Wall Street futures, and markedly lower closes overnight on Asian exchanges.

The August personal consumption expenditures core price index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation metric, is slated for an 8:30 am ET release. Analysts project a 2.9% year-over-year rise, unchanged from July's gain.

In economic news, European consumers in August expected prices to rise 2.8% over the following 12 months, up from a 2.6% rise expected in July, the European Central Bank reported.

The pan-continental Stoxx Europe 600 Index was up 0.3% mid-session.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Technology Index was down 0.7%, while the Stoxx 600 Banks Index gained 0.8%.

The Stoxx Europe 600 Oil and Gas Index was up 0.3%, while the Stoxx 600 Europe Food and Beverage Index was 0.1% lower.

The REITE, a European REIT index, gained 0.2%, and the Stoxx Europe 600 Retail Index was up 2%.

On the national market indexes, Germany's DAX was up 0.3%, and the FTSE 100 in London advanced 0.4%. The CAC 40 in Paris was 0.4% higher, and Spain's IBEX 35 gained 0.6%.

Yields on benchmark 10-year German bonds were lower, near 2.75%.

Front-month North Sea Brent crude-oil futures were up 0.1% at $69.47 a barrel.

The Euro Stoxx 50 volatility index was down 2% at 16.98, indicating below-average volatility for European stock markets in the next 30 days, a positive signal. A reading above 20 indicates choppier markets ahead, while below 20 suggests calmer exchanges.

data.title: Fitch Maintains Eiffage Rating on Solid Investment-Grade Profile
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Fitch reiterated Eiffage's (FGR.PA) short-term issuer default rating at F2, according to a Friday release.

The rating affirmation highlights the French construction engineering company's solid investment-grade profile, "stronger" volume risk, "midrange" price risk and debt structure, as well as its strong liquidity ratio.

The action also reflects the low-volatility traffic of Eiffage's motorways operating unit APRR, the minimal contribution from its other concessions businesses, and its solid and broad contracting activity, among others.

data.title: Sanuwave Health Refinances $27.5 Million Debt With New JPMorgan Credit Facility
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Sanuwave Health (SNWV) said Friday that it has refinanced its $27.5 million debt with NH Expansion Credit Fund by securing a new credit facility from JPMorgan (JPM).

The new credit facility consists of a four-year term loan of $23 million and a two-year $5 million revolving credit facility, both of which have an interest rate of secured overnight financing rate plus 350 basis points, the company said.

Sanuwave said it fully repaid the NH Expansion debt facility using proceeds from the new credit facility and existing cash.

Shares of Sanuwave were up more than 4% in recent premarket activity Friday.

data.title: Fed's Barkin: low confidence in any inflation forecast right now - BBG Television
data.description:
Washington, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Thomas Barkin said he had very low confidence in inflation forecasts right now as tariffs continue to impact the economy and businesses and households wrestle over who will bear the cost of new import taxes.

"There are cost increases suppliers want to pass on. There's no question about that," Barkin said on Bloomberg Television. "But those costs are going to attempted to be passed on to a consumer who is frankly exhausted with price increases...That may mean that you won't see as much broad based inflationary impact."

data.title: South Korea's Business Sentiment Rises to 10-Month High in September
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- South Korea's business sentiment in September rose to its highest in 10 months as chip manufacturing improved amid delays in the U.S. tariff deals.

The Composite Business Sentiment Index climbed to 91.6 from 91 in the prior month, the highest level since November 2024, according to Friday data from the Bank of Korea.

On a month-on-month basis, the manufacturing sector increased to 93.4 from 93.3, while the non-manufacturing sector climbed to 90.5 from 89.4.

An official from the central bank said semiconductors led the rise in the manufacturing sector during the month, despite uncertainties hounding the talks with the U.S. over its tariffs, The Korea Times reported Friday.

The official also said the state-based consumer coupons and other stimulus measures helped boost domestic demand, which drove the non-manufacturing sector higher, the report said.

Meanwhile, the outlook for the country's business sentiment for October, as well as for the manufacturing and non-manufacturing sectors, all dropped.

data.title: Singapore Manufacturing Output Contracts in August
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Singapore's total manufacturing output in August fell 7.8% on year and declined a seasonally adjusted 9.7% from July, reported the city-state's Economic Development Board (EDB) on Friday.

Excluding the volatile biomedical manufacturing sector, August factory output decreased 2.9% on year and fell 3.5% from July, added officials.

August marked the first on-year decline in total output since March 2024, according to EDB figures.

Singapore's biomedical manufacturing sector reported output was down 37.3% year-on-year in August, led by a 59.3% decline in pharmaceuticals.

The production of medical devices, however, gained 5.3% on year in the month, reported the EDB.

In contrast, Singapore's transport engineering manufacturing segment output increased 18.9% on year in August, due in part to higher production of aircraft parts and sustained maintenance, repair and overhaul work for commercial airlines.

Chemical production in the city-state rose 1.7% on year in August, while that of precision engineering goods fell 1.7%, added the EDB.

In the electronics sector, output declined 4.8% in August on year, although the information, communication and consumer electronics segment expanded 42.4% on the higher output of server-related products. However, semiconductor output fell 8.8% on year, in the month.

Singapore's general manufacturing segment logged output down 13.9% on year in August, reported the EDB.

In mid-August, Singapore's Ministry of Trade and Industry upgraded its 2025 gross domestic product growth forecast to 1.5% to 2.5%, from the previous 0.0% to 2.0% range, citing strong AI-related production and an easing of trade tensions.

data.title: Indonesia agency says lack of oversight in free meal programme led to  food poisoning cases
data.description:
JAKARTA (Reuters) -An Indonesian government agency running President Prabowo Subianto's massive free meals programme said on Friday its lack of oversight led to a recent outbreak of food poisoning among thousands of school children.

Concerns have mounted over the standards and supervision of the multi-billion-dollar initiative. Nearly 6,000 people have fallen ill after eating the free lunches since the programme's January roll-out, government data showed, including more than 1,000 children in the country's West Java province this week.

The programme has been rapidly expanded to reach more than 20 million recipients, with an ambitious goal of feeding 83 million by the end of the year. The agency said on Friday it estimated it could only spend 99 trillion rupiah ($5.92 billion) this year out of the 171 trillion rupiah initially allocated for the scheme.

Nanik Deyang, the National Nutrition Agency's deputy head, apologised for the recent cases, breaking down in tears.

"Our biggest mistake is ... our lack of oversight," she said, adding that the agency takes full responsibility. "The recent cases ... were because our partners and our internal teams did not follow the right standard operating procedures."

Inconsistent cooking times contributed to the poisoning, she said, adding that the agency would step up supervision.

Processed foods have been banned from the lunches, and kitchens lacking proper health certification will be shut down by next month, she said.

The West Java cases were linked to kitchens using spoiled food, and Deyang noted a shortage of nutritionists across the archipelago.

Investigations into the poisoning cases have started, involving food regulators and the police, Nanik added.

Health organisations have previously called for the suspension of the programme. West Java Governor Dedi Mulyadi urged an evaluation, warning that the cases could overwhelm small local hospitals.

When asked about the calls to suspend the programme entirely, Nanik said about 40 kitchens lacking proper standards certificates had been closed.

($1 = 16,725.0000 rupiah)

(Reporting by Ananda Teresia; Writing by Stanley Widianto; Editing by Louise Heavens)
